front: Define Capacity Planning in IT context
back: The process of determining the IT resources (hardware, software, network) needed to meet current and future workload demands at acceptable performance levels.
The number of tasks or transactions processed per unit of time, typically measured in requests per second (RPS), transactions per second (TPS), or operations per second (OPS).
The time delay between a request being initiated and the response being received. Lower latency indicates better performance.
The total time taken to complete a single request or transaction, including processing time, queue time, and network delay.
The percentage of time a resource (CPU, memory, disk, network) is actively processing work relative to its maximum capacity.
A measurement of normal system performance under typical workload conditions, used as a reference point for comparing future performance.
The process of running standardized tests to measure system performance and compare it against industry standards or other systems.
A point in the system where capacity is limited, causing performance degradation. The slowest component that limits overall system throughput.
A contract defining expected service levels including availability, response time, throughput, and penalties for failing to meet commitments.
Mean Time Between Failures - the average time a system operates before experiencing a failure, measuring reliability.
Mean Time To Repair/Recovery - the average time required to restore a failed system to operational status.
The ability of a system to handle increased workload by adding resources (vertical) or nodes (horizontal) without performance degradation.
The percentage of time a system is operational and accessible, typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.9% = three nines).
The process of analyzing and describing the nature of work submitted to a system, including request types, frequencies, and resource requirements.
A predefined limit for a metric; when exceeded, it triggers alerts or automated responses to prevent performance issues.
